code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about astronauts (or related entities) into a
    single fluent sentence. The function maps known predicates to natural‑language
    phrases, strips surrounding quotes from objects, and joins the resulting
    clauses with commas and a final “and”. If no triples are provided an empty
    string is returned.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicates to more natural wording.
    pred_map = {
        "almaMater": "studied at",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was born as",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired in",
        "occupation": "worked as",
        "status": "has status",
        "timeInSpace": "spent",
        "mission": "participated in the mission",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "was a",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as chief of the astronaut office in",
        "title": "held the title",
        "ribbonAward": "received the ribbon award",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by",
        "crewMembers": "had crew member",
        "representative": "was represented by",
        "alternativeName": "also known as",
        "awards": "received",
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senators",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received award",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    def clean_obj(obj: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes and extra whitespace."""
        obj = obj.strip()
        if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        return obj

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        # Choose a readable predicate phrase; fallback to the raw predicate.
        phrase = pred_map.get(t.predicate, t.predicate.replace("_", " "))
        obj = clean_obj(t.object)

        # Special handling for timeInSpace to add units.
        if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
            clause = f"{t.subject} {phrase} {obj} minutes in space"
        else:
            clause = f"{t.subject} {phrase} {obj}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine clauses into a single sentence.
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]

    # Capitalize first character and ensure a period at the end.
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of triples about astronauts (or related entities) into a
      single fluent sentence. Each triple is turned into a simple clause
      “subject predicate object”. Clauses are combined with commas and an
      “and” before the final clause.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      clauses = []
      for t in triples:
          # Make predicate more readable: replace underscores with spaces
          pred = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
          # Simple clause construction
          clause = f"{t.subject} {pred} {t.object}"
          clauses.append(clause)
  
      if len(clauses) == 1:
          sentence = clauses[0]
      else:
          sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
  
      # Capitalize first character and add a period.
      sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
      if not sentence.endswith("."):
          sentence += "."
      return sentence
